hero.title: Time Zone Scheduler
hero.description: Compare one meeting time across participant time zones and save lightweight schedules in your browser.

flow.title: Create -> Compare -> Save
steps: Create a meeting schedule with a base date, time, and time zone.
Add participant time zones and choose the zone marked as My Time.
Compare local times, save the schedule, and review it later.

differences.title: Fast global planning without server scheduling
differences.description: Time Zone Scheduler focuses on local comparison instead of calendar integrations, invitations, or notification services.

items.title: Time-zone clarity
items.description: See the same meeting moment in each participant's local time.

items.title: My Time highlight
items.description: Mark the user's primary zone so the comparison is easier to scan.

items.title: Local privacy
items.description: Keep saved schedules in browser storage without backend calendar or invitation services.

feel.title: A compact meeting-time comparison board
feel.description: The workflow keeps creation, saved schedules, and local insights close together.

items.title: Create in a drawer
items.description: Add the meeting details without leaving the scheduling view.

items.title: Compare at a glance
items.description: Review participant local dates and times with My Time highlighted.

items.title: Revisit locally
items.description: Search, edit, duplicate, or delete saved schedules in the browser.

audiences.title: Who uses Time Zone Scheduler
audiences.description: Useful for people coordinating calls across regions without needing a calendar integration.

items.title: Remote teams
items.description: Plan collaboration windows across distributed teammates.

items.title: Recruiters
items.description: Compare interview times for candidates and panels in different regions.

items.title: Freelancers
items.description: Coordinate client calls while keeping proposed times easy to understand.

resourceSection.title: Scheduling essentials
resourceSection.description: A focused local workflow for meeting-time comparison.

items.title: Schedule
items.description: Title, base date, base time, and base time zone.

items.title: Participants
items.description: Multiple IANA time-zone names included in the comparison.

items.title: Insights
items.description: Local totals, unique zones, most-used zone, average participants, and recent schedules.

groupedSection.title: Plan across time zones without extra services
groupedSection.description: Move from schedule setup to saved comparisons and local insights.

items.name: Scheduler
items.description: Create schedules through the shared form drawer.

items.name: My Time
items.description: Highlight one zone as the user's primary reference.

items.name: Schedules
items.description: Search, edit, duplicate, delete, or clear saved browser-local schedules.

items.name: Insights
items.description: Review local scheduling patterns without backend persistence.

technicalSection.title: Browser-local by design
technicalSection.description: Time Zone Scheduler follows the shared Ansiversa mini-app architecture while avoiding backend scheduling in V1.
items: Lazy-loaded frontend module with shared overview, authenticated page state, page header, form drawer, empty state, feedback, record action, and stats components.
No calendar SDK, scheduler library, backend scheduling, notifications, invitations, cloud sync, or heavy time-zone dependency.

finalCta.title: Explore browser-local time-zone planning
finalCta.description: Create a schedule, compare participant local times, and keep useful plans in this browser.
